claim: REQ-003's supersession list is incomplete: a SIXTH frozen test pins SCHEMA_VERSION === 7 - TEST-008 in tests/main/quick-store-toasts.test.ts:41-43 (feature 0001 REQ-007, header: 'TEST-008 pins the current SCHEMA_VERSION constant value'). The spec's Verified contract claims the five-test list is the 'full list' ('grep verified'), but the pre-flight grep missed this one. Under REQ-002's v8 bump TEST-008 goes RED with no scheduled amendment, making REQ-003's own acceptance ('the full frozen-suite run is green at F9's implementation gate') unattainable except via the silent implementation-phase edit of a frozen test that REQ-003 itself forbids - the exact F6 FINDING-001 / wrong-pinned-upstream-fact failure class this review was tasked to catch. (Verified exhaustive by repo grep: exactly six literal toBe(7) SCHEMA_VERSION pins exist - the five listed plus TEST-008; workspace-model/split-direction/app-state-model tests compare symbolically and are safe.)
fix: Add TEST-008 (tests/main/quick-store-toasts.test.ts, feature 0001 REQ-007) to REQ-003's normative supersession list as a sixth (b)-class amendment (re-pin at 8 with a supersession note naming F9 REQ-003), correct the Verified contract's 'full list', and update REQ-003's acceptance to say six tests. propose CONV: A spec claim of an exhaustive enumeration of colliding frozen tests ('full list', 'grep verified') MUST state the exact grep pattern and scope used, and the pattern MUST target the pinned literal value (e.g. toBe(7)) repo-wide, not a list of already-known files.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-003: the six-guard inventory now includes TEST-008 (quick-store-toasts.test.ts:41-43) with the grep patterns documented in the Verified contract; independently re-verified by repo grep (exactly six SCHEMA_VERSION toBe(7) pins, two documented unrelated hits). Proposed CONV recorded (#2).

claim: REQ-015's acceptance contradicts REQ-007's normative mandate (the TEST-324 failure class). REQ-007 REQUIRES the detail payload to be 'the UNMODIFIED orkyFeatureStatus(...) output' with per-finding blocking computed by 'openBlockingCount's per-entry predicate'; the Definition of Done classifies payload assembly as one of F9's pure modules ('Pure logic ... payload assembly field-by-field'); yet REQ-015's acceptance mandates a code assertion that 'the component and F9's pure modules contain no gateFrontier/isStalled/openBlockingCount/ORKY_AUTONOMOUS_PHASES/inPopover/orkyFeatureStatus( reference'. An implementation satisfying REQ-007 necessarily fails REQ-015's scan and vice versa. Compounding: openBlockingCount has NO exported per-entry predicate - it is an inline loop body (orky-status.ts:134-143, verified) - so 'reuse the predicate' is unimplementable without either forking it (violating REQ-015's 'never a re-implementation') or an orky-status.ts refactor the spec nowhere schedules.
fix: Scope REQ-015's acceptance scan to the component and F9's renderer PRESENTATION modules only (the payload assembler is explicitly exempt - it is the sanctioned mapper-pipeline caller). Pin the blocking-predicate seam: schedule exporting a per-entry isBlockingFinding(f) predicate from src/shared/orky-status.ts (a shared-file touch - note it in REQ-013's shared-files list and keep CONV-012 in view) and have openBlockingCount delegate to it, OR sanction a local predicate with an equivalence test against openBlockingCount over a generated vector set.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-007/REQ-013/REQ-015: isBlockingFinding is scheduled as an exported orky-status.ts extraction (verbatim from the :134-143 loop body) with openBlockingCount delegating, equivalence-tested; REQ-015's scan is re-scoped to the component + renderer presentation modules with the main-side assembler explicitly exempt; orky-status.ts added to REQ-013's shared-files list. Verified no frozen test pins openBlockingCount structurally (behavior-level tests only), so the delegation breaks nothing.

claim: REQ-010's closed trigger set ('exactly these triggers and no others', no polling, no timer, no manual refresh anywhere in the spec) keys refresh solely on deep-unequal changes to the bound root's AGGREGATE entry - but that entry is lossy exactly along the dimensions the detail channel exists to carry (D3: 'what the aggregate deliberately omits'). Verified stale classes: (a) findings.json changes that preserve openBlockingCount (adding/resolving MEDIUM/LOW findings, editing claims, resolving one HIGH while opening another) change no OrkyFeatureStatus field, so the entry stays value-identical; (b) ANY change to an idle or clean-done feature - the very features REQ-009 exists to surface - is invisible because inPopover (orky-status.ts:309-314) excludes them from status.features entirely (e.g. a gate passing on a non-active feature: gateN advances on disk, entry unchanged); the slice's deep-equal short-circuit (registry-slice.ts:77-87) then suppresses even the push notification. Result: the pane renders stale findings/gates/escalation detail INDEFINITELY while looking fresh and healthy, with no recovery path until an unrelated roll-up-visible change happens - defeating the intake's 'show me EVERYTHING about THIS project' for precisely the data classes the pane adds over the chip.
fix: Close or explicitly accept-and-document the hole in REQ-010: add a T3 trigger (e.g. a visible-pane low-frequency revalidation, a user-facing refresh affordance in the pane header, or a cheap main-side per-root change fingerprint - e.g. a monotonic counter bumped on every engine reread emit regardless of roll-up value - carried on the entry so ANY .orky change under the root is renderer-visible). At minimum the spec MUST name the staleness classes it accepts and surface a manual refresh so the user is never stuck. propose CONV: A detail view whose refresh triggers key on changes to a LOSSY summary/roll-up MUST have an independent currency mechanism (or an explicit staleness contract) for the data the summary omits - a summary-keyed trigger can only refresh what the summary can see.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-022 (+ the REQ-010 rewrite): registry:rootChanged, a per-root notification on EVERY completed engine re-read, is now the detail view's currency mechanism; both verified staleness classes are pinned as REQ-022 acceptance vectors. Re-verified the wiring premise: the engine emits unconditionally with no delta gating (orky-root-engine.ts:169-171, called at :188 and :233), OrkyRegistry's engine subscription keys dirname(orkyDir) (orky-registry.ts:42-45), and the registrar's one-line push pattern (register-registry.ts:61) supports the pinned send. No renderer subscription leak: one App.tsx-level subscription (torn-down block, App.tsx:44-91) into one store action, per-pane state pruned via clearPaneRuntime (REQ-017). Proposed CONV recorded (#3).

claim: Torn-read behavior for the one-shot detail read is unpinned. Orky's state.json writes are NON-atomic (gatekeeper.js writeJson = plain writeFileSync, :54-56; only findings.json gets writeJsonAtomic temp+rename, :319-323). The engine's watcher path is shielded by awaitWriteFinish (150 ms stability) + 300 ms debounce (orky-root-engine.ts:108-117), but REQ-008's one-shot detail read has no stability discipline and its triggers (mount, re-bind, own-entry change) correlate with write activity. Under mandated engine parity, a torn state.json read silently DROPS that feature from an ok:true payload (parity with :215-217) - a vanished row presented as truth, which REQ-011's 'a FAILED refresh keeps rendering the stale-but-valid detail' clause does not cover because the fetch SUCCEEDS. Combined with REQ-010's closed triggers, if the completing write leaves the roll-up entry value-identical, the wrong payload persists indefinitely. The spec also pins no arbitration or staleness signal for a detail response disagreeing with the aggregate-derived header rendered beside it.
fix: Pin the torn/partial-read behavior in REQ-008: e.g. the detail assembler compares the settled payload against the pane's previously-held payload and (a) retries once after a short delay when a previously-present feature disappeared or a state.json parse failed, and/or (b) carries a per-feature 'skipped: unreadable' marker instead of silent omission so REQ-009 can render it distinctly. Pin which surface wins when header (aggregate entry) and body (detail payload) disagree, and how/if staleness is signaled.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-008(b)/REQ-009/REQ-011: a parse-failed existing file retries exactly once after 150 ms; a still-failing state.json is surfaced via skippedFeatures (rendered as orky-pane-feature-unreadable), findings.json via findingsUnreadable; an ok:true payload is never silently shorter than the tree; arbitration pinned (the detail payload wins for everything displayed, single clock). Non-atomic writeJson pin re-verified at gatekeeper.js:54-57.

claim: REQ-007's claim that the payload's status 'can never contradict the chip/aggregate for the same bytes' is false in production: orkyFeatureStatus takes an injected now, the aggregate entry was computed at the last fs-event-driven engine reread, and the detail payload computes at fetch time. A stall-threshold crossing between the two (isStalled: now - lastTickAt > 120s, orky-status.ts:163-176) yields header 'busy' and rows 'stalled - needs you' from IDENTICAL bytes - and the disagreement is DURABLE, because a stall by definition produces no fs event, so no new push ever updates the entry or re-triggers the fetch (REQ-010). The 'stalled Xm' minute count in detail (:243) likewise diverges across clocks. The acceptance vector ('the feature's status deep-equals the aggregate's entry for the same fixture bytes') only holds with an identical injected clock, so the over-broad normative claim is untestable as written - the acceptance-inconsistent-with-normative-rule (TEST-324) class.
fix: Scope the no-contradiction claim to 'for the same bytes AND the same injected clock', and pin how the pane treats time-sensitive divergence: e.g. the pane's header roll-up signals derive from the detail payload's own carried statuses (one clock, self-consistent pane) rather than the aggregate entry, with the aggregate used only for membership/source; record the aggregate's fs-event-gated stall blindness as an upstream F5 note alongside the existing Open-questions items.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-007/REQ-009: the no-contradiction claim is scoped to same bytes AND the same injected clock (computedAt carried as the one clock instant); REQ-009 pins header-from-detail arbitration (aggregate supplies only membership/source/unbound transition) plus a no-renderer-clock source scan; the aggregate's fs-event-gated stall blindness is recorded as upstream F5 note (Open questions #5).

claim: Cap-selection nondeterminism: REQ-008 mandates 'the SAME MAX_FEATURE_DIRS (200) cap' with engine parity, but the engine applies the cap to the UNSORTED readdir enumeration (orky-root-engine.ts:200-203 - slice before any sort), so for a tree with >200 feature dirs WHICH 200 features survive depends on fs enumeration order. That contradicts REQ-014's 'payload order MUST be deterministic and independent of readdir/fs enumeration order' (the SET, not just the order, becomes enumeration-dependent) and REQ-007's 'the payload MUST be a pure function of the on-disk bytes', and undermines REQ-007's acceptance that the feature's status deep-equals the aggregate's entry (the two readers can cap different subsets). No REQ pins the cap's selection order for the detail path.
fix: Pin in REQ-008 that the detail read sorts the slug list by codepoint BEFORE applying the MAX_FEATURE_DIRS cap (deterministic survivor set), and state explicitly whether the aggregate's enumeration-order cap >200 is accepted as a documented divergence (with REQ-007's deep-equal acceptance scoped to <=200-feature fixtures) or fixed engine-side in the same change. propose CONV: An enforced cap/truncation over a filesystem enumeration MUST be applied to a deterministically-ordered list, never to raw readdir order, so which items survive the cap is a pure function of the on-disk state.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-008(a)/REQ-014: the detail read sorts the slug list by codepoint BEFORE the cap (with a 201-dir shuffled-enumeration acceptance vector); the engine's raw-order cap is explicitly accepted as a flagged upstream F5 divergence (Open questions #4, not bundled); REQ-007's aggregate-equivalence acceptance is scoped to <=200-feature fixtures. Proposed CONV recorded (#4).

location: 02-spec.md Verified contract (findings.json pinned shape) / REQ-007 acceptance; .orky/features/0006-decision-queue-panel/findings.json
claim: The Verified contract's pinned findings.json shape omits contract_violation - yet the real 0006 ledger the spec claims to pin against contains it (verified: key union includes contract_violation), and REQ-007's own blocking predicate consumes it ('open AND (CRITICAL/HIGH OR contract_violation === true)'). Under the spec's own rule 'If a datum is not listed here, F9 does not display it', the blocking mark's third input is formally unpinned. REQ-007's acceptance vectors also never exercise the OR-branch: only 'a blocking:true open-HIGH vector and a blocking:false resolved-HIGH vector' are required, so an implementation that computes blocking from severity alone (dropping the contract_violation arm - the branch the Gatekeeper blocks on regardless of severity) passes every acceptance test.
fix: Add contract_violation?: boolean to the Verified contract's pinned findings.json shape, and add an acceptance vector to REQ-007: an OPEN finding with severity MEDIUM and contract_violation: true MUST map to blocking: true (and a resolved contract_violation one to blocking: false).
resolution: Resolved: contract_violation?: boolean is now in the pinned findings.json shape, citing the real 0006 ledger occurrences (re-verified present at lines 110/276/291/306), and REQ-007's acceptance adds both OR-branch vectors (open-MEDIUM contract_violation:true -> blocking:true; resolved contract_violation -> blocking:false).

claim: D2's realization silently omits a third new-pane creation affordance: the split compass (SplitMenu.tsx) carries an enumerated Terminal/Editor/Explorer kind selector (:145-150) - a per-kind creation surface every existing pane kind has and D2's 'command palette entry and wherever new-pane affordances live' plausibly covers. REQ-004's justification 'the palette + select are the parity surfaces' is factually incomplete as a parity claim (editor/explorer each have THREE creation surfaces), and the spec neither adds an orky option to the compass nor records its exclusion as a resolved decision - so a keyboard user can split-create every kind in a chosen direction EXCEPT orky, and the omission looks accidental rather than decided.
fix: Resolve explicitly in the spec: either (a) add an 'orky' kind button to SplitMenu's kind group routing through the same injected root-picker flow (noting SplitMenu's cwd-coupled explorer precedent for a disabled state when no snapshot member exists), or (b) record the exclusion in Resolved spec-time decisions with a rationale (e.g. compass kinds are cwd-contextual; orky needs a picker modal that fights the compass's transient popover) and correct the 'parity surfaces' wording so the frozen spec doesn't misstate the existing affordance set.
resolution: Resolved by decision #4(c)/REQ-004 via option (a): SplitMenu's kind group gains an 'orky' button (split-kind-orky-<paneId>, aria-pressed parity) with the explorer/cwd disabled precedent (SplitMenu.tsx:120 re-verified) routing through the shared picker; direction-split commit and cancel semantics pinned. The load-bearing 'compass e2e passes unchanged' claim independently re-verified: TEST-009/010/016 and quick-commands.test.ts:8 are all open-form (per-kind assertions / arrayContaining, no closed-set or count pin).

claim: The root picker's boundary states are underspecified: REQ-004 pins vectors for a 3-member snapshot and a held [] snapshot, but registrySnapshot === null (no snapshot applied yet - loading, per the slice's derived-loading rule at registry-slice.ts:51 - or a held registryError) is a DISTINCT state the spec never addresses. Rendering the 'how roots become tracked' empty copy during a mere loading window or a registry error mis-describes the system state (the F6 REQ-011/012/013 loading-vs-empty-vs-error discipline, and CONV-002's boundary-behavior rule, applied to F9's own new surface).
fix: Pin picker behavior for the null-snapshot and registryError cases in REQ-004: a distinct loading state (or disabled creation until the first snapshot settles) and an error state surfacing the slice's specific registryError text (CONV-001), each with an acceptance vector, mutually distinct from orky-root-picker-empty.
resolution: Resolved by decision #5/REQ-004: four mutually-distinct picker states - loading keyed on the slice's derived rule (registrySnapshot === null && registryError === null, registry-slice.ts:51 re-verified verbatim), error surfacing registryError verbatim (CONV-001), empty only for a genuinely-held [], and the member list - each with acceptance vectors including mutual exclusivity and the compass button's disabled-while-loading behavior.

claim: Three phase-4 test defects block the implement gate independent of implementation correctness: (1) orky-pane-structure.test.ts:184 has a syntax error (missing ')' before '{' in 'as const {') so TEST-428..439 never execute; (2) TEST-397 seeds case-only-distinct dirs (b-feat/B-feat) that collapse on case-insensitive NTFS — three expected features can never materialize from two physical dirs; (3) TEST-401 same trap — 201 case-colliding seeds materialize as 101 dirs so featuresCapped:true is unreachable. Implementer mirrored TEST-428..439 assertions out-of-band: all pass.
fix: Tests loopback: fix the syntax character; replace case-only-distinct fixture names with codepoint-distinct ones (e.g. B-feat/a-feat/b2-feat; 201 f-NNN slugs) preserving each test's pinned property
resolution: Sanctioned tests loopback: syntax char fixed (TEST-428..439 now execute and pass, confirming the implementer's mirrored evidence), TEST-397/401 re-fixtured with codepoint-distinct names preserving their pinned properties. Full suite 1229/1229 green. propose CONV recorded in 04-tests.md (on-disk fixtures must not rely on case-only-distinct paths).

claim: The registry:detail assembler (new in F9) guards against symlinked FEATURE DIRECTORIES (lstat check at lines 197-199, engine parity) but never lstat-checks the individual data files it reads inside a tracked root: active.json (line 177), state.json (line 201), and findings.json (line 211) are all opened via readJsonOnce's plain stat()+readFile() (lines 59-69), which follows symlinks. A tracked project (e.g. a cloned/untrusted repo) whose .orky/active.json, .orky/features/<slug>/state.json, or .orky/features/<slug>/findings.json is a symlink pointing outside the project root has its target read, JSON-parsed, and selectively echoed into the registry:detail payload -- and, unlike the pre-F9 aggregate (which only ever surfaced computed booleans/counts), F9 renders rich VERBATIM string fields from that payload straight into the OrkyPane UI (finding claim/evidence, escalation reason/decision, gate evidence -- OrkyPane.tsx lines 116,128,136,149-166). This is a CWE-59 (Improper Link Resolution Before File Access) information-disclosure oracle: an attacker who can place one file inside a directory the victim later tracks as an Orky project can read and partially exfiltrate arbitrary JSON-shaped files elsewhere on disk (any file the Electron main process can read) into the victim's own UI, without ever gaining code execution. No test in tests/main/orky-root-detail-bounds.test.ts or orky-root-detail.test.ts exercises a symlinked state.json/findings.json/active.json (only a symlinked feature directory, TEST-406) -- confirmed by grep.
fix: Extend the existing feature-dir symlink guard to the three leaf files: before each readJsonStable/readJsonOnce call on active.json, state.json, and findings.json, lstat the target path and treat isSymbolicLink() the same as a 'torn'/unreadable file (surface via skippedFeatures/findingsUnreadable, never follow the link) rather than passing straight to stat()+readFile(). Add TEST vectors mirroring TEST-406 for a symlinked state.json, findings.json, and active.json (each pointing outside the fixture root) asserting the target's content never reaches the payload.
resolution: Fixed: all three leaf files lstat-checked in the detail path (orky-root-detail.ts:60-80), symlinks refused unfollowed and surfaced per file class. DA cross-confirmed. Residual noted: dedicated leaf-symlink test vectors not added (dir-symlink TEST-406 only) — recorded for the residual ledger.

claim: The 1 MiB MAX_FILE_BYTES bound (REQ-008) is enforced via a separate stat() call followed by a separate readFile() call on the same path (readJsonOnce, lines 60-68) -- a classic CWE-367 TOCTOU gap. Because the detail read is explicitly a one-shot reaction to on-disk writes from a NON-atomic producer (the file's own doc comment at lines 10-15 and the spec's Verified contract both note gatekeeper.js writes state.json/active.json via plain writeFileSync, not temp+rename), a file that is small at stat() time and grows past 1 MiB before the immediately-following readFile() bypasses the size cap entirely, defeating the bound REQ-008 exists to guarantee and allowing an unbounded read into the Electron main process's memory from a single tracked root.
fix: Close the TOCTOU window: open the file once (fs.open), fstat the open file descriptor to get size, enforce MAX_FILE_BYTES against that fstat result, then read from the SAME descriptor (fs.read/createReadStream) rather than a fresh path-based readFile -- or read with a byte-limited stream that aborts once MAX_FILE_BYTES is exceeded regardless of the file's growth after the initial stat.

claim: An OrkyPane in an INACTIVE (background) workspace is treated as displayed and fetches full detail on EVERY own-root registry:rootChanged notification. App.tsx keeps every workspace mounted under a visibility:hidden + aria-hidden + pointer-events:none host (App.tsx:160-172 - the exact hiding mechanism the spec's Verified contract cites as THE displayed/hidden boundary for MinimizedPaneHost), but PaneTile mounts OrkyPane with no hidden prop (PaneTile.tsx:148); only MinimizedPaneHost passes hidden (MinimizedPaneHost.tsx:46). So during a busy Orky run (engine notifications up to ~3.3/s per root at the 300ms debounce), every background-workspace pane bound to an active root sustains a continuous fetch loop the user cannot see: a full .orky walk main-side per fetch (up to 200 feature dirs, ~5 sequential fs calls each, plus 150ms torn-read retry delays), the IPC payload transfer, two store writes and re-renders of an invisible up-to-200-row pane - multiplied by every background workspace with a bound pane. This defeats REQ-010 T3's entire purpose (hidden panes mark stale and fetch ONCE on display) and contradicts REQ-010's MUST ('fetch on exactly these triggers and no others; T2 ... while the pane is DISPLAYED', where 'Displayed = rendered in the VISIBLE mosaic tile' - a background workspace's tile is not visible). Root cause is a false spec premise: REQ-010 T3's parenthetical claims a workspace switch UNMOUNTS panes ('closed workspace view, remount on workspace switch'), but App.tsx deliberately keeps all workspaces mounted (App.tsx:155-159 comment, a pre-F9 shipped invariant the Verified contract never pinned), so the workspace-activity dimension of the displayed/hidden boundary was never specified, tested (TEST-423 covers only the MinimizedPaneHost case), or implemented.
fix: Thread workspace activity into the existing hidden boundary: PaneTile (or WorkspaceView) passes hidden={workspace not active} into OrkyPane - setOrkyPaneHidden then marks background-workspace panes stale on own-root notifications and REQ-010 T3's restore-fetch fires once on workspace activation, exactly like minimize/restore. Amend REQ-010's false unmount premise, and add trigger vectors for the background-workspace case (own-root notification while the workspace is inactive fetches zero times and marks stale; switching to the workspace fetches exactly once). propose CONV: A displayed/hidden gate on a per-pane runtime behavior MUST key on the pane's EFFECTIVE visibility (every keep-mounted-but-hidden host: minimized host AND inactive-workspace host), never on just one named hiding surface.
resolution: Resolved by the REQ-010 amendment + fix: PaneTile threads hidden={activeId !== wsId} into OrkyPane (PaneTile.tsx:48,155 — value-stable selector) and MinimizedPaneHost keeps passing hidden; the slice's setOrkyPaneHidden owns both hosts' boundary (TEST-446/447; e2e TEST-466 drives background-workspace fixture churn → activation refresh end-to-end). Adversarially re-checked: undocked windows are correct — each window is its own renderer store (applyAssignment hydrates only that window's workspaceIds with its own activeId, store.ts:182), so wsInactive is per-window by construction; minimized+inactive combos are correct — MinimizedPaneHost mounts per workspace with hidden always true, a restore into a still-inactive workspace lands hidden={wsInactive}=true, and activation then drives T3 (fetch iff stale). Residual found: MAXIMIZE hides sibling tiles with the same visibility:hidden pattern (index.css:282-283) and is covered by neither host — filed as FINDING-035 (spec-origin: the amendment enumerates exactly two hidden hosts).

claim: Every refresh re-renders the pane TWICE, not once: issue() patches { root, inFlight: true, pendingRefetch: false } into the same orkyPaneDetail entry object the component subscribes to (useStore(s => s.orkyPaneDetail[paneId]), Object.is equality), producing a new entry reference at issue time and again at settle - yet NONE of the bookkeeping fields (root/inFlight/pendingRefetch/stale/hidden) affects rendered output (OrkyPane reads only entry.detail and entry.error; TEST-430 even pins that). REQ-016's acceptance pins 'an A-root notification -> one fetch, then exactly ONE re-render on settle'; the implementation produces two full component passes (each reconciling the up-to-200-row list; the orderedFeatures memo prevents re-sorting but not reconciliation), i.e. ~6 render passes/sec instead of 3 on a visible pane during a busy run. The gap is also a test-adequacy hole: REQ-016 is covered only by slice-level reference-stability for OTHER-root notifications (TEST-422); none of the acceptance's own-root render-count vectors exists ('exactly one re-render on settle', 'a push changing A's entry -> exactly one re-render pass', 'a settled fetch triggers one derivation (spy)'), which is why the double-render passed the implement gate. [MERGE NOTE: absorbs FINDING-034 — REQ-016's one-render-on-settle acceptance was never encoded as a test.]
fix: Move the fetch bookkeeping that drives no render (inFlight, pendingRefetch - and stale/hidden if kept out of render forever) into slice-closure maps beside the existing `generations` Map, patching the subscribed store entry only when detail or error actually changes; alternatively have the component subscribe narrowly (useShallow pick of { detail, error }). Add the missing REQ-016 render-count/derivation-spy vectors so the one-render-per-settle contract is pinned.

claim: CONV-011 letter violation: the slice-closure `generations` Map (pane id -> fetch generation) is never pruned and has no dispose-clear - clearPaneRuntime prunes the pane's orkyPaneDetail entry (internals.ts:52-58, verified routed from close, workspace close, AND the move-away/applyAssignment path) but cannot reach the closure Map, so every orky pane that ever fetched leaves a permanent entry for the renderer's lifetime. The code comment claims it is 'self-cleaning: keys are only ever pane ids that fetched', which bounds who ENTERS the map, not who leaves it. No stale-value hazard exists (generations are monotonic per id and settle() independently guards on entry existence), and each entry is a string+number, so this is growth-only - but CONV-011 is explicit that a per-key map mirroring a live membership set MUST prune keys that leave membership.
fix: Prune the pane's generation in the same seam that prunes its orkyPaneDetail entry (e.g. expose a pruneOrkyPaneRuntime(paneIds) from the slice that clearPaneRuntime's callers invoke, or store the generation on the entry itself so CONV-011's existing pruning covers it).

claim: One row's disclosure toggle re-renders the ENTIRE feature list: expandedRows is component-level state and featureRow is an inline render function (no memoized row component), so toggling one disclosure re-runs React reconciliation for all rows - up to the 200-feature cap, including every OTHER expanded row's full gates/findings/escalations subtree (findings arrays can be large). Keys are correct and stable (slug / finding id / phase), the orderedFeatures sort is memoized on the payload reference, and the DOM diff is minimal, so the cost is bounded and paid only at user-gesture frequency - but it compounds with the double-render-per-refresh (see the sibling MEDIUM finding) on busy roots: every settle also reconciles the full list including expanded subtrees. Non-blocking; recorded because the row set can hit the 200 cap with deep expanded content.
fix: Extract a React.memo'd FeatureRow component taking (feature, expanded, onToggle-with-stable-identity) so a disclosure toggle re-renders only the toggled row, and unchanged feature objects skip reconciliation entirely on refreshes where their payload subtree is reference-equal.

claim: Restore-from-minimize issues TWO registry:detail fetches, not one, contradicting REQ-010's/REQ-017's pinned acceptance ('restore fetches exactly once' / 'restore fetches once — REQ-010 T3') and the shipped doc's own claim (docs/features/orky-pane.md:100, 'fetch exactly once on restore'). MinimizedPaneHost.tsx's own comment confirms minimize/restore is a genuine unmount+remount ('the source unmounts, and this mount re-adopts'), not a continuous instance toggling a hidden prop -- PaneTile mounts OrkyPane fresh with hidden defaulting to false. On that remount both useEffects fire in the same commit: the T3 hidden-boundary effect (OrkyPane.tsx:59) runs first and, since the persisted orkyPaneDetail entry survived the remount with stale:true, calls setOrkyPaneHidden->fetchOrkyDetail, which synchronously sets entry.inFlight=true via issue() (orky-pane-slice.ts:95-104); the T1 mount-trigger effect (OrkyPane.tsx:64-67) then runs unconditionally (it does not check whether a stale-restore fetch was just issued) and calls fetchOrkyDetail again, hitting the coalescing branch (orky-pane-slice.ts:106-114) which queues pendingRefetch:true -- settle()'s tail (line 92) then fires a second registryDetail(...) IPC round-trip once the first settles. No test catches this: TEST-423 (tests/renderer/orky-pane-slice.test.ts:154-173), the only test exercising this exact scenario, calls setOrkyPaneHidden directly at the slice level and never also invokes fetchOrkyDetail the way the T1 effect would on the same remount, so the two effects' real interaction is never simulated; REQ-010's traceability (traceability.json REQ-010: TEST-420-424, TEST-430) contains no component-mount or e2e test of the actual MinimizedPaneHost->PaneTile transition with a registryDetail call-count spy.
fix: Coordinate the two effects instead of firing independently: e.g. merge them into one effect that first resolves the hidden->displayed transition (issuing the stale-restore fetch when entry.stale is true) and only falls through to the T1 mount-fetch path when no stale-restore fetch was just issued this commit; or have fetchOrkyDetail's coalescing recognize 'a fetch for this exact root was just synchronously issued in this tick' as a no-op rather than a queued follow-up. Add a component or e2e-level test that mounts OrkyPane hidden, marks it stale via notifyOrkyRootChanged, unmounts it from MinimizedPaneHost and mounts it fresh via PaneTile (matching the real transition), with a registryDetail call-count spy asserting exactly one call.
resolution: Merged into FINDING-020 — same restore double-fetch defect; 020 carries the contract flag and the non-stale-restore branch.

claim: OrkyRegistry gains a second private method, memberSpelling (feature 0009, REQ-006's membership gate for registry:detail), that duplicates resolveCanonical's scan almost verbatim: both iterate 'for (const existing of this.paneRoots.values())' then 'for (const existing of this.persistedRoots)', comparing normalizeProjectRoot(existing) === normalizeProjectRoot(root) in both loops -- the only difference is the final fallback (resolveCanonical falls back to resolve(root); memberSpelling falls back to null). This is a real reuse-discipline gap: memberSpelling is the sole gate deciding whether registry:detail performs a filesystem read for a renderer-supplied root string (REQ-006: 'any other value... gets root-not-tracked... so the channel can never be used to read arbitrary filesystem paths'), so keeping it in lockstep with resolveCanonical's matching semantics by copy-paste rather than shared code is a maintenance risk: if the membership-source scan ever changes (e.g. a third membership source is added, or the fold/normalization semantics change), a future editor who updates resolveCanonical and misses the second, independent copy in memberSpelling would silently desync which roots the detail channel considers members.
fix: Factor the shared scan into one private helper, e.g. private findTrackedSpelling(root: string): string | null that does the normalizeProjectRoot + dual-loop lookup and returns null on no match; have resolveCanonical become 'return this.findTrackedSpelling(root) ?? resolve(root)' and memberSpelling become 'return this.findTrackedSpelling(root)' (or inline memberSpelling's role directly into detail() via the shared helper). This removes the duplicated loop while preserving each caller's distinct fallback behavior.
resolution: Fixed: single findTrackedSpelling scan (orky-registry.ts:266-275); resolveCanonical and memberSpelling delegate with their distinct fallbacks preserved. DA cross-confirmed.

claim: OrkyRootPicker.tsx (new in F9) duplicates two distinct pieces of DecisionQueuePanel.tsx presentation logic rather than sharing them, despite REQ-015's explicit 'reuse, don't fork' mandate for this feature's substrate. (1) The loading/error boolean derivation is copy-pasted verbatim: DecisionQueuePanel.tsx:109-110 'const loading = registrySnapshot === null && registryError === null; const failed = registrySnapshot === null && registryError !== null' reappears identically at OrkyRootPicker.tsx:46-47 as 'loading'/'failed' -- now THREE independent restatements of the slice's derived loading rule exist in the renderer (registry-slice.ts's own derivation, DecisionQueuePanel's, and OrkyRootPicker's), none sharing a single exported selector, so a future change to what 'loading' means requires editing all call sites in lockstep with nothing enforcing agreement. (2) The CONV-020 open-focus/close-restore effect is duplicated near line-for-line: DecisionQueuePanel.tsx:33-45 (capture prevFocus, focus a ref on mount, on cleanup check 'document.activeElement === null || === document.body' and conditionally restore) is structurally identical to OrkyRootPicker.tsx:35-44 (same capture/focus/cleanup-guard/restore shape, only variable names and the fallback-selector differ). CONV-020 codifies the BEHAVIOR contract but nothing shares the IMPLEMENTATION, so a later bugfix or edge-case correction to this hard-won focus pattern (born from FINDING-010/FINDING-023 in 0006) must be applied to both components by hand, with no guarantee the second copy is remembered.
fix: Extract two shared pieces: (a) a small exported selector/hook in registry-slice.ts (or a co-located helper), e.g. useRegistryLoadState() returning { loading, failed }, consumed by both DecisionQueuePanel and OrkyRootPicker (and any future registry-snapshot consumer); (b) a shared useOpenFocusRestore(containerRef, fallbackSelector) hook encapsulating the CONV-020 open/close focus-management effect, parameterized by the fallback target (the '[data-testid="orky-queue-toggle"]' query for the drawer vs the picker's captured prevFocus-only path), used by both components instead of two hand-maintained copies.
resolution: Fixed: shared use-registry-load-state and use-open-focus-restore hooks consumed by DecisionQueuePanel + OrkyRootPicker; SplitMenu keeps a deliberate inline derivation (frozen TEST-455 pins the literal there). DA cross-confirmed.

claim: REQ-010's restore-fetch discipline is violated end-to-end. Restore from minimize is a cross-host REMOUNT (the pane stays mounted while MINIMIZED in MinimizedPaneHost, but restore moves it into PaneTile's subtree — the host's own doc says 'before the source unmounts, and this mount re-adopts'), and OrkyPane's T1 mount effect (OrkyPane.tsx:64-67 — `hidden` in deps, no restore gating) fires on every restore. Verified consequences: (a) a restore with NO suppressed notification fetches ONCE where REQ-010's acceptance pins ZERO ('restore with no suppressed notification fetches zero times'); (b) a stale restore fetches TWICE — effect :59's setOrkyPaneHidden issues the T3 stale fetch, then effect :64's fetchOrkyDetail coalesces into a pendingRefetch follow-up that re-issues on settle (two registryDetail IPC calls = two full <=200-dir .orky walks) — where REQ-010's acceptance and REQ-017's acceptance ('restore fetches once — REQ-010 T3') pin EXACTLY ONE. The counts are deterministic but contradict REQ-010's MUST ('fetch detail on exactly these triggers and no others'; T3: 'a stale pane fetches exactly once'). It slipped because TEST-423 pins these vectors at the SLICE seam only (driving setOrkyPaneHidden directly, where the discipline holds) and orky-pane-structure.test.ts is source-scan only — the acceptance's end-to-end component-lifecycle vector is never exercised. [MERGE NOTE: absorbs FINDING-017 (quality) and FINDING-030 (devils-advocate): every re-bind also fetches twice; the acceptance pins zero on non-stale restore / exactly one on stale restore or re-bind.]
fix: In OrkyPane, capture the entry's pre-flip hidden state at mount (wasHidden = getState().orkyPaneDetail[paneId]?.hidden === true) and skip the T1 fetch for a restore transition (wasHidden === true — setOrkyPaneHidden already owns the fetch-iff-stale decision), keeping the T1 fetch for fresh mounts and workspace-switch remounts (no entry, or entry.hidden === false) and for re-bind/membership re-entry; add a component-lifecycle test (remount with the hidden->displayed host move) asserting the pinned 0/1 fetch counts. propose CONV: A pinned trigger/count discipline whose boundary is a component lifecycle transition (mount/unmount/hidden->displayed) MUST be tested through the real component lifecycle, not only through the store/slice seam beneath it — a slice-level spy cannot see fetches the component's own mount effects add.
resolution: Resolved by the merged mount/hidden effect (OrkyPane.tsx:75-83): the surviving entry is consulted via getState() BEFORE the hidden flip; wasHidden routes every hidden→displayed transition exclusively to T3 (setOrkyPaneHidden fetches iff stale), and the inFlight+sameProjectRoot guard prevents the re-bind duplicate. Trace-verified counts against the amended matrix: fresh mount 1, non-stale restore 0, stale restore 1, re-bind 1 (rebindOrkyPane's own fetch; the root-dep effect re-run hits the in-flight guard), membership re-entry 1 (hidden re-entry heals via the re-track notification's stale mark). Pinned by TEST-450 (structural getState consult) + TEST-447/423 slice counts + e2e TEST-466, per the coordinator's sanctioned two-way pinning. Residuals — dev-only StrictMode double-invocation breaks the non-stale-restore 0-count, and no real-lifecycle minimize→restore call-count vector exists anywhere — filed as FINDING-036 (LOW).

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyPane.tsx:92-99 (key={s.feature}, expandedRows[s.feature]); src/main/orky/orky-root-detail.ts:227-233 (OrkyFeatureDetail carries no dir slug)
claim: Feature rows and disclosure state are keyed by status.feature, which is NOT unique within a payload: the wire shape drops the readdir dir slug (unique by construction), and status.feature comes from state.json's `feature` field with the '(unknown)' fallback. A copied feature dir (e.g. `0006-x` plus a `0006-x.bak` backup — same feature field) or two malformed state.json (both collapsing to '(unknown)') therefore produce duplicate React keys (React's duplicate-key reconciliation is contractually undefined — rows can be dropped or duplicated, silently breaking REQ-009's 'EVERY feature' rendering on that input class), a SHARED expandedRows disclosure entry (toggling one row expands/collapses both — the disclosure state is stable but not uniquely keyed), and duplicate data-feature row identities (REQ-012's stable (projectRoot, featureSlug) identity is no longer unique for F10's action targeting). The payload itself stays deterministic (stable sort ties fall back to codepoint-sorted slug insertion order); the nondeterminism enters at React reconciliation.
fix: Carry the readdir dir slug on OrkyFeatureDetail (unique by construction) and key the row, the expandedRows entry, and data-feature by it; alternatively suffix the render/disclosure key with the payload index as a stable tiebreak. Add a duplicate-feature-field fixture vector (two dirs, same `feature` value) to the payload/render suites.
resolution: Resolved: the wire carries the unique dir slug (orky-root-detail.ts:251-254), payload order gains the slug codepoint tiebreak (:265-267 — a total order under duplicate feature fields), and the component keys the row, the expandedRows disclosure entry and data-feature on f.slug (OrkyPane.tsx:137-148, aria-labels included); display-order ties fall through the stable sort to the slug-tiebroken payload order, so duplicate feature values stay deterministic end-to-end and REQ-012's (projectRoot, featureSlug) identity is unique by construction. TEST-392/453/459/460 green; REQ-012/REQ-014 amended to pin it.

claim: The per-pane fetch-generation Map is fetch bookkeeping keyed by paneId that is never deleted: clearPaneRuntime prunes orkyPaneDetail but cannot reach this slice-closure map, so a closed pane's generation entry outlives its pane — contra REQ-017's letter ('EVERY per-pane runtime entry F9 adds (orkyPaneDetail, any fetch/staleness bookkeeping) MUST be pruned through the SAME clearPaneRuntime seam ... so no entry outlives its pane'). The code comment claims the map is 'self-cleaning', which it is not. Behaviorally benign — verified: the guard stays monotonic under paneId reuse (issue() always increments from the stored value, so an inherited higher generation can never admit a stale settle, and a pruned entry's settle early-returns at :77 before the generation check matters); footprint is one number per ever-fetched paneId — so this is a hygiene/letter deviation, not a violation of the REQ's protective intent (no stale inheritance can manifest), hence LOW and no contract flag.
fix: Prune the generation entries alongside the state seam: either move the generation into the orkyPaneDetail entry itself (so clearPaneRuntime's existing delete covers it; settle compares against the entry's generation) or expose a slice-level dropOrkyPaneRuntime(paneIds) invoked wherever clearPaneRuntime is applied; correct the 'self-cleaning' comment either way.
resolution: Merged into FINDING-015 — same generations-map pruning gap; 015 filed first.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyRootPicker.tsx:53-62 (dialog-level onKeyDown) + :106 (Cancel button inside the handled subtree)
claim: The picker's dialog-level key handler is not target-guarded (REQ-018: 'any row-level key handling MUST target-guard so nested controls' keys are never swallowed' - the F6 FINDING-008 class): keydown bubbles from EVERY nested control to the dialog div, whose Enter branch preventDefaults (suppressing native button activation) and calls onSelect(memberRoots[sel]). Verified from source: (1) a keyboard user who Tabs to the Cancel button and presses Enter COMMITS A PANE bound to the sel-highlighted root instead of cancelling - a direct violation of REQ-004's 'Cancel/Escape MUST commit no pane in every state'; (2) Enter on a Tab-focused option button selects memberRoots[sel] (default 0), not the visibly focused option, so the WRONG root can be committed; (3) in the empty/error/loading states Enter on the Cancel button does nothing at all (preventDefault kills activation; memberRoots is empty). Adequacy gap that let this pass: TEST-444/TEST-445 press Enter only while the listbox container holds focus - no vector Tabs to a nested control first, so the frozen suite pins only the happy keyboard path.
fix: Guard the ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Enter branches on the event target being the listbox itself (e.g. `if (e.target !== listRef.current) return` before the selection keys; keep Escape dialog-wide), and reconcile the dual keyboard model: either remove the option buttons from the Tab order (roving tabindex) or drive selection from real DOM focus; add aria-activedescendant + option ids so the container-focus listbox is AT-perceivable. Add a keyboard vector that Tabs to Cancel and presses Enter, asserting zero panes committed. propose CONV: A container-level key handler that intercepts activation keys (Enter/Space) MUST target-guard so a nested native control's own activation is never suppressed or redirected - Enter on a focused button must activate THAT button.
resolution: Resolved by the target-guarded handler (OrkyRootPicker.tsx:49 — Arrow/Enter act only when e.target === listRef.current; Escape stays dialog-wide; the empty/loading/error Enter path commits nothing via the memberRoots clamp+guard): Enter on a Tab-focused Cancel cancels in every state, Enter on a Tab-focused option commits THAT option. Pinned by TEST-456 (source guard) + TEST-464 e2e (both matrix vectors, non-default option targeted). Space/Home/End verified hole-free (unhandled keys keep native activation/scroll, never a commit); the container-focus listbox path is unbroken (tabIndex 0, clamped sel). Residual, spec-sanctioned, non-blocking: the dual keyboard model remains without aria-activedescendant — REQ-018 as amended mandates target-guarding only, and the Tab path (real buttons, aria-selected, named by root) is fully AT-operable.

claim: Two items in REQ-009's mandated display set never render: (1) the per-gate `at` timestamp - the payload carries it (OrkyGateDetail.at, pinned tz-safe epoch ms) but the component never references g.at anywhere; the gate span's title falls back to evidence or a pass/fail phrase, so the MUST-listed 'pass/fail/unrecorded + at + external marker' is exposed 2-of-3; (2) header stall wording - REQ-009 mandates the header carry 'the project roll-up signals (needs-human/failed accents, stall wording)', but only the needs-you/failed accents render; the payload's carried stall wording (reason 'stalled' / detail 'slug: stalled Xm - no heartbeat') never reaches the header, so a stalled project's header is indistinguishable from an awaiting-human-review one. Adequacy gap that let this pass: the renderer suite is source-scan-only (TEST-428/429 grep testids), the e2e suite never expands a feature row, and only the MAIN-side payload test (tests/main/orky-root-detail.test.ts:90) pins g.at - in the payload, never in the DOM.
fix: Expose g.at in the gate disclosure (e.g. in the span's title alongside evidence) and surface the comparator-top feature's carried stall wording in the header when its reason is 'stalled' (render the carried detail/reason verbatim - REQ-015). Mind the pinned-scan tension: TEST-430 bans `new Date(` in the component, so format the carried epoch main-side at assembly or via a sanctioned pure formatter - formatting a carried epoch is deterministic; re-deriving elapsed time with a renderer clock is the thing REQ-009 forbids.
resolution: Resolved: gateTitle exposes the carried at via formatOrkyInstant (OrkyPane.tsx:128-133 — pure UTC epoch formatter in shared/orky-pane.ts, no renderer clock; unrecorded gates invent nothing) and the header renders the comparator-top stalled feature’s carried detail verbatim (OrkyPane.tsx:118-119,247-253), distinguishable from an awaiting-review header. Pinned by TEST-451/TEST-452 (clock ban re-asserted in-suite); both green. Noted for the coordinator: the two pins are source-scan-shape per the node-env harness convention — the DOM-level two-fixture distinctness vector REQ-009’s acceptance words exists only at that seam.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyPane.tsx:203 (failed accent; needs-fallback drift also at :116,119,137,143,154,202,223,231,238) vs 02-spec.md REQ-019
claim: REQ-019 mandates the pane's needs-human/failed accents use 'the established status colors'; the header's failed accent instead uses var(--status-fail, #e05050) - a variable defined NOWHERE (theme.ts's themeCssVars/VAR_OF map no --status-fail; index.css defines none), so it always renders the novel literal #e05050 and no app/workspace/per-pane theme override can ever reach it. The established failure token is --status-failure/#c62828, used by the term-failure toolbar treatment (index.css:59) and the failed Orky CHIP's outline (index.css:64-66) - so the SAME project shown failed in the chip and in the F9 pane renders two different reds (the drawer/pane coexistence-consistency angle). Secondary drift in the same family: the needs accents use the correctly theme-mapped --status-needs but with a non-standard #e0a030 fallback (the token's standard fallback is #ff8f00 - index.css:11,50; Toasts.tsx:8). TEST-439's hex scan only asserts hexes sit inside var() fallbacks, so a dead variable name with a novel fallback passes it - the accent is effectively a hard-coded color the scan cannot catch.
fix: Use var(--status-failure, #c62828) for the failed accent and align the --status-needs fallbacks to the token's standard #ff8f00. propose CONV: A component consuming a theme/status CSS variable MUST use a variable name actually defined by the theme system or a :root rule, with that token's standard fallback - a var() with an undefined name plus a novel fallback literal is a hard-coded color that a hex-in-fallback source scan cannot catch.
resolution: Resolved: the failed accents now use var(--status-failure, #c62828) (OrkyPane.tsx:180,246) and every needs accent uses var(--status-needs, #ff8f00); the dead --status-fail/#e05050 and the novel #e0a030 fallback are gone from F9 files (repo grep — remaining #e0a030 sites are pre-F9 --status-needs-input consumers, established via index.css:311). Pinned by TEST-462 (repo-wide var-validity — re-run green, no other dead var snuck in) and TEST-463 (F9 status-token fallbacks byte-matched to #ff8f00/#c62828/#1e88e5).

location: src/renderer/components/Modal.tsx:62-68 (unconditional deferred refocusActivePane) vs src/renderer/components/OrkyRootPicker.tsx:35-44 (the CONV-020 conditional restore) - REQ-004
claim: REQ-004 pins CONV-020's close half for the picker ('on close focus is restored ONLY if it is still inside the picker'), and OrkyRootPicker.tsx:35-44 implements the conditional restore correctly - but the Modal wrapper it mounts in unconditionally schedules refocusActivePane() when the last modal closes (Modal.tsx:66), which requestPaneFocus-es the previously focused pane. Whenever that pane has a registered focuser (terminals - the only kind that registers one, TerminalPane.tsx:51), the picker's just-restored focus is yanked away a microtask later. Concrete flow: split compass -> orky kind -> direction -> picker -> Escape: SplitMenu.close() restores focus to the split trigger, the picker's cleanup preserves it, then the microtask moves focus into the terminal - the 'never unconditionally / never yanking' behavior CONV-020 exists to prevent, and inconsistent with the compass's own Escape (which leaves focus on the trigger). The re-bind and palette flows escape only accidentally (orky panes register no focuser; the palette opener is already unmounted). No test can see it: close-time focus is asserted nowhere (the node suite is source-scan; TEST-445's Escape vector asserts only pane count).
fix: Suppress the Modal's deferred refocus when the closing overlay already placed focus deliberately (e.g. skip refocusActivePane when document.activeElement is not body/null at microtask time - the same collapsed-focus test the picker itself uses), or give Modal an opt-out the picker sets; add a close-time focus assertion to the compass-flow e2e.
resolution: Resolved: Modal’s deferred refocusActivePane is now behind the collapsed-focus guard (Modal.tsx:70-74 — fires only when activeElement is null/body at microtask time), so a deliberately-restored close focus is never yanked. Spot-checked the two riskiest of the 8 consumers myself: SearchHistory reveal (Reveal unmounts with the card → focus collapses → refocus fires against the just-revealed focusedPaneId, end state unchanged; when requestPaneFocus already landed, the skip is a redundant-call elision) and SettingsPanel (no autoFocus: every close path either collapses to body → refocus fires as before, or leaves focus on a real surviving control — the CONV-020-correct outcome). Pinned end-to-end by TEST-465 (compass → picker → Escape leaves focus on split-<paneId> after the microtask settles).

claim: The compass's disabled orky kind button carries ONE accessible name for two distinct states: 'Orky (disabled: no tracked Orky project yet - open a terminal in a project containing .orky/ to track one)' renders both for a genuinely-empty registry AND while the snapshot has simply not settled (hasOrkyMember folds null and [] together at :39). During the startup window a user WITH tracked projects is told none exist - the loading-vs-empty mis-description the picker itself was required to keep distinct (REQ-004/FINDING-009 discipline), surviving on a smaller surface. The implementation follows spec decision #4c verbatim (it sanctioned lumping 'null or empty' under one explanatory name), so this is a spec-time wording gap, not an implementation error.
fix: Distinguish the two in the accessible name (e.g. 'Orky (waiting for tracked projects...)' while registrySnapshot === null && registryError === null), mirroring the picker's own loading copy; one-line amendment to decision #4c's copy.
resolution: Resolved by amended decision #4c/REQ-004: SplitMenu now derives three mutually-distinct disabled names (SplitMenu.tsx:46-52 — loading keyed on the slice’s derived rule registrySnapshot === null && registryError === null; a held-error name; the genuinely-empty copy ONLY for a held []). The third (error) wording is an implementer addition beyond the spec’s two — assessed correct under ux: the spec’s ONLY-held-[] constraint leaves the error state no compliant name otherwise, and it mirrors the picker’s own four-state discipline (verbatim error text stays available via the picker, appropriate for a disabled button’s accessible name). Pinned by TEST-455 (loading keying + loading-class wording + empty copy).

claim: readJsonOnce classifies ANY stat() failure as 'absent': an existing state.json/findings.json that cannot be stated (EACCES/EPERM, permission-broken tree) is silently treated as missing — state.json absence silently skips the feature (line ~202) and findings absence reads as readable-empty (~211-213) — violating REQ-008's MUST that unreadable state.json surfaces in skippedFeatures and unreadable findings.json sets findingsUnreadable, never a silently-shorter ok:true payload
fix: Distinguish ENOENT/ENOTDIR from other stat failures in readJsonOnce: true absence stays 'absent'; any other stat error returns 'torn' so it flows through the retry + skippedFeatures/findingsUnreadable surfacing path
verified_by: codex-cross-check (filed by coordinator after line-level verification; codex runtime ran read-only and could not write the lock)
resolution: Resolved: readJsonOnce classifies only ENOENT/ENOTDIR as absent; every other stat/read failure is torn → bounded retry → surfaced (state.json → skippedFeatures, findings.json → findingsUnreadable) (orky-root-detail.ts:73-79,86). Matches the amended REQ-008(b) exactly: absence is legitimate and unretried (a dir without state.json stays a non-feature, engine parity); an EXISTING-but-unreadable file is never silently classified as missing, and the coverage invariant (features ∪ skippedFeatures covers every dir whose state.json exists) holds under EACCES/EPERM. TEST-457/458 green, with the fs mock denying stat, open AND readFile so the pin is independent of the syscall sequence.

claim: Re-bind renders the PREVIOUS root's held detail as the NEW root's data - REQ-010's acceptance ('a response settling after the pane re-bound to root C is discarded... DOM shows C's loading/detail, never A's') and REQ-009's single-source rule ('the detail payload wins for everything the pane displays') are violated on the ONLY reachable re-bind flow. Sequence (verified by trace): pane bound to A holds a settled ok:true payload; A leaves membership (unbound state, held detail retained per design); user re-binds to C via orky-pane-rebind. rebindOrkyPane -> issue() patches { root: C, inFlight: true } but never clears `detail` (orky-pane-slice.ts:98), and the component derives okDetail from detailEntry.detail with NO okDetail.root-vs-config.root guard (OrkyPane.tsx:69-70). Until C's fetch settles - and INDEFINITELY if it fails (the error renders inline while the held detail keeps rendering, REQ-011 path) - the pane renders C's basename/header over A's payload: A's feature rows, A's needsHuman/failed header accents, A's activeFeature, each row stamped data-project-root=C with A's slugs - the exact (projectRoot, feature) identity REQ-012 pins for F10's action dispatch, now a cross-root mislabel (an F10 action would target root C + a feature of A). The frozen suite cannot catch it: TEST-424 drives the slice with detail still null (asserts null stays null, never a HELD prior payload) and never renders DOM; the structure suite is source-scan only.
fix: Gate the rendered payload on binding identity: in OrkyPane, treat okDetail as held ONLY when sameProjectRoot(okDetail.root, config.root, {caseFold}) - otherwise render orky-pane-loading (the payload carries its root precisely for this). Additionally (belt and braces) have issue()/the coalesce path null out `detail` (and `error`) whenever the newly targeted root does not sameProjectRoot-match the held detail's root, so a failed C-fetch can never leave A's payload as the persistent render source. Add a slice+scan vector: held-A-payload -> rebind C -> assert detail is not renderable as C's before the C settle.
resolution: Resolved twice over: rebindOrkyPane clears held detail/error at the slice BEFORE the fresh fetch unless the held ok-payload sameProjectRoot-matches the new binding (orky-pane-slice.ts:158-170), and the component render-guards the held payload on its CARRIED root (heldDetail, OrkyPane.tsx:86-93), so even an uncleared path cannot render A's rows under C's identity (data-project-root/data-feature stay coherent for F10). Attacked with rapid A→C→A: the C-step clears A's payload (C's loading renders — fresh-over-held, correct), C's late settle is discarded via stillCurrent, the coalesced follow-up refetches A (latest root wins) and repopulates; a failed refetch surfaces the specific error over loading, never the old root's payload; no permanent blanking. TEST-449 (slice) + TEST-454 (render guard) green.

claim: The minimize/restore transition violates REQ-010's pinned fetch counts because restore is a REMOUNT between hosts (MinimizedPaneHost -> PaneTile are different React parents; the host's own doc pins 'before the source unmounts, and this mount re-adopts') and OrkyPane's mount effect (OrkyPane.tsx:64-67) fetches unconditionally on every mount with a bound member root. Verified by trace of the two effects in declaration order: (1) restore with NO suppressed notification = 1 fetch (setOrkyPaneHidden(false) patches hidden only, then the mount effect fetches) - the spec pins ZERO ('restore with no suppressed notification fetches zero times'); (2) stale restore = 2 fetches (setOrkyPaneHidden's T3 fetch goes in-flight, the mount effect then coalesces a pendingRefetch that issues a second IPC read after settle) - REQ-010 T3 and REQ-017's acceptance pin EXACTLY ONCE; (3) every re-bind = 2 fetches (rebindOrkyPane's direct fetch + the root-changed mount-effect re-run coalescing a redundant follow-up), violating the one-trigger-one-fetch discipline ('a trigger arriving mid-flight schedules exactly ONE coalesced follow-up' - here ONE trigger produces a fetch AND a follow-up). The frozen suite is blind to all three: TEST-423/424 exercise the slice seam only (no component mount semantics), and the e2e vectors count no fetches. The entry's own state distinguishes the cases at mount time (a restore-remount sees the pre-existing entry with hidden:true; a workspace-switch T1 remount sees hidden:false), so the spec's trigger model is implementable as written - the component just fetches on every mount instead of only on genuine bind events.
fix: Make the mount/bind effect entry-aware: on mount, consult the pane's existing orkyPaneDetail entry (getState() at event time, CONV-021) and fetch only when (a) no entry exists, (b) the entry's root does not sameProjectRoot-match config.root, or (c) membership re-entered - and let setOrkyPaneHidden's T3 path own the stale-restore fetch exclusively (fold the two effects into one so ordering cannot double-issue). Drop the redundant direct fetch inside rebindOrkyPane OR exclude `root` changes from the mount effect when an in-flight fetch for that root already exists. Add slice-level counts driven through a mount-simulation harness (setOrkyPaneHidden(false)+fetchOrkyDetail in the component's real order) pinning: non-stale restore = 0, stale restore = 1, rebind = 1.
resolution: Merged into FINDING-020 — same remount fetch-count defect found independently; 020 absorbs 030's every-re-bind-fetches-twice branch (noted in 020 by this merge).

claim: A coalesced follow-up fetch fires from a HIDDEN pane: settle() issues the pendingRefetch follow-up checking only `after.root !== ''` - never `after.hidden`. Sequence (verified): displayed pane, own-root notification during an in-flight fetch -> pendingRefetch=true; pane is minimized before the settle (entry.hidden=true); the settle then issues a fresh registryDetail read while the pane is hidden - contradicting the spec's performance contract ('hidden/unbound panes never fetch', Concerns) and the T3 design (a hidden pane's currency signal is the `stale` mark, not a fetch). Same gap for membership loss mid-flight: the follow-up fires for a now-unbound root and settles a structured root-not-tracked result over the held detail. Root cause is a SPEC gap: REQ-010 pins the notification-while-hidden case (T3) but never pins the coalesced follow-up's interaction with a hidden/unbound transition landing mid-flight, and no acceptance vector covers it - so the frozen suite (TEST-421/423) cannot catch it. Bounded impact (exactly one extra one-shot read per race window), but it is the letter-violation of the hidden-never-fetches discipline the parent trigger model exists to guarantee.
fix: Pin the interaction in REQ-010's concurrency paragraph: a pendingRefetch settling into a hidden entry MUST convert to `stale: true` (fetch nothing); one settling into an unbound/non-matching binding MUST be dropped. Implement in settle(): `if (after?.pendingRefetch && after.root !== '') { if (after.hidden) patchEntry(paneId, { pendingRefetch: false, stale: true }); else issue(paneId, after.root) }`. Add the minimize-mid-flight vector to the slice suite.
notes: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (devils-advocate): PARTIALLY fixed — left open for the residual. The headline branch is closed: settle()'s tail converts a pendingRefetch settling into a HIDDEN entry to stale:true and issues nothing (orky-pane-slice.ts:96-100; TEST-448). The membership-loss branch remains: nothing in production ever sets entry.root to '' (membership is snapshot-derived in the component and never propagated to the slice), so the tail's unbound drop is unreachable-in-production, and a follow-up settling after mid-flight membership loss still issues a fetch for the now-non-member root whose refused root-not-tracked result then REPLACES the held detail — exactly the originally-claimed behavior; notifyOrkyRootChanged likewise has no membership gate for a post-removal race notification (matrix row 4 "ZERO fetches" unmet for the reachable variant). TEST-448's unbound leg manufactures root='' by direct state mutation — a state production cannot reach. Impact re-assessed: one wasted IPC read per rare race window plus a held-detail overwrite invisible behind the unbound render, self-healing on re-entry — severity downgraded MEDIUM→LOW. Residual fix: inject a membership predicate into the slice deps (consulted in notifyOrkyRootChanged and the settle tail), or propagate membership loss into the entry.

claim: REQ-020's guarantee 'downstream code never sees a non-string binding' is false for the SECOND persisted instantiation path: workspace templates. The coercion seam lives only in deserializeWorkspace (normalizeOrkyBindings), but templates persist in quick.json - whose loader validates nothing per-template (quick-store.ts:21, `Array.isArray(v.templates) ? v.templates : []`) - and workspaceFromTemplate/remapPaneIds clone configs opaquely with no normalization. A hand-edited quick.json template containing { kind: 'orky', root: 42 } (or root absent) - exactly the hostile-hand-edit threat model REQ-020 itself exists for - instantiates a pane whose non-string binding reaches OrkyPane, where `basenameOf(root)` calls `root.split(...)` unconditionally at render time (OrkyPane.tsx:87) and THROWS, crashing the renderer subtree - strictly worse than the graceful unbound degrade REQ-020 mandates on the workspace path (CONV-002). Verified: no template-path coercion exists anywhere (grep), and sameProjectRoot tolerates the non-string (foldPath returns null) so the crash lands only at basenameOf. Spec origin: the spec asserted a single load-normalization seam while two persisted instantiation paths exist; templates are in-scope surface (REQ-001's acceptance pins template round-tripping of orky panes).
fix: Coerce at the template-instantiation seam too: workspaceFromTemplate (or remapPaneIds) applies the same non-string-root -> '' coercion for kind:'orky' configs; additionally make OrkyPane total over its config (basenameOf guards typeof root === 'string', rendering unbound for anything else). Add a vector: a template with root:42 instantiates an unbound pane, no throw. propose CONV: A load-time coercion that guarantees 'downstream never sees X' MUST be applied at EVERY deserialization/instantiation path of that persisted shape (workspace files, templates, imports/duplications), not only the primary loader - or the guarantee must name the paths it covers.
resolution: Resolved: normalizeOrkyBindings is applied at BOTH persisted instantiation paths — deserializeWorkspace (workspace-model.ts:181, migrated output included) and workspaceFromTemplate (:300) — and the component is total over its config (root typeof guard OrkyPane.tsx:42; basenameOf guards non-string, :312-315). Attacked for further paths: undock/redock transfer carries workspaceIds only and re-hydrates via main's loadWorkspace → deserializeWorkspace (renderer store.ts:182 + persistence/store.ts:32), so it rides the coerced loader; live creation (pane-ops.ts:45) takes the picker's member-root string; templateFromWorkspace serializes from in-memory already-coerced state; no drag-import/duplication path exists. TEST-461 green (root:42 and absent-root templates land unbound with no throw; the well-formed sibling passes byte-verbatim).

claim: Cross-FILE read coherence within one feature is a spec hole (bounded, self-healing): REQ-008 pins per-FILE torn-read behavior only, so an ok:true payload can mix generations - state.json read after a gate write but findings.json read before the paired ledger write (or vice versa; the producer writes them from separate commands, state.json non-atomically via plain writeFileSync, findings.json via temp+rename - verified against the live gatekeeper.js:54-57/319-323). The assembler reads the two files sequentially per feature with independent 150 ms retries, widening the interleave window. Verified mitigation: the skew is transient and self-healing - each producer write fires the watcher -> debounce -> completed re-read -> REQ-022 rootChanged -> displayed panes refetch (hidden panes mark stale and fetch on restore), so a skewed payload survives at most one notification cycle (~450 ms) while displayed. Also noting: readJsonStable's retry treats a torn-then-DELETED state.json as 'absent' and drops the feature from both features and skippedFeatures - acceptable only because the dir is then genuinely mid-deletion (the coverage invariant is vacuous), worth a code comment. Neither behavior is pinned or documented as an accepted staleness class, which the spec's own FINDING-003 resolution standard ('an explicit staleness contract for what the mechanism cannot see') requires.
fix: Document, do not redesign: add a note to REQ-008 (and docs/features/orky-pane.md) that an ok:true payload is coherent per-file, not per-feature-across-files, and that REQ-022's per-write notification is the healing mechanism bounding the skew to one notification cycle; add a comment at readJsonStable pinning the torn-then-absent outcome. Optionally add a vector: write findings.json mid-assembly and assert the NEXT notification-driven read converges.
resolution: Resolved by documentation as prescribed: REQ-008's accepted-staleness paragraph and docs/features/orky-pane.md (Accepted staleness class — cross-FILE read skew) both record per-FILE coherence, the producer's split write disciplines, and the notification-driven healing chain (write → watcher → 300 ms debounce → completed re-read → rootChanged → displayed refetch / hidden stale-mark), with the ~450 ms bound appropriately hedged; readJsonStable's comment pins the torn-then-absent outcome (orky-root-detail.ts:90-96). Wording re-checked for accuracy: the at-most-one-notification-cycle claim holds because the trailing paired write itself fires a notification (the engine emits unconditionally, no delta gating). TEST-467 pins the doc; REQ-021 makes it mandatory.

claim: REQ-016's acceptance 'an A-root notification -> one fetch, then exactly one re-render on settle' is not met and not pinned: issue() eagerly patches { root, inFlight: true, pendingRefetch: false } into the render-subscribed orkyPaneDetail entry, so every fetch produces TWO entry-reference changes (issue-time + settle-time) and therefore two render passes of the pane, even though nothing rendered consumes inFlight/pendingRefetch. Test adequacy gap alongside it: the node-env harness cannot count React renders, so the suite substituted reference-stability at the slice seam (TEST-422, other-root case only) - the own-root re-render count is unverified anywhere, which is how the extra pass survived the gate. Impact is one wasted render pass of a single pane per fetch (negligible), hence LOW - filed because it is a pinned acceptance the suite structurally cannot check.
fix: Keep render-consumed fields (root, detail, error, stale, hidden) in the store entry and move pure fetch bookkeeping (inFlight, pendingRefetch) into slice-scope maps beside `generations` (they drive no render), OR skip the issue-time patch when root is unchanged. If left as-is, amend REQ-016's acceptance to 'at most one ADDITIONAL render pass at issue time, none of it observable in DOM output' so the contract matches what the harness can enforce.
resolution: Merged into FINDING-014 — same issue-time second-render defect; 014 filed first (034 adds that the acceptance was never encoded — noted).

claim: MAXIMIZE is a third keep-mounted-but-hidden surface the amended REQ-010 misses. The amended spec asserts the shipped renderer has exactly TWO keep-mounted-but-hidden hosts (minimized host, inactive-workspace host) and threads only those into the pane hidden boundary - but .ws-max hides EVERY sibling tile with visibility:hidden + pointer-events:none (index.css:282-283; the CSS comment itself calls it the same visibility:hidden pattern as inactive workspaces), while PaneTile computes hidden from workspace activity alone (hidden={wsInactive}, PaneTile.tsx:48,155). An OrkyPane sibling of a maximized pane in the ACTIVE workspace is therefore effectively invisible while treated as displayed: every own-root registry:rootChanged notification sustains a full detail fetch (up to ~3.3/s per root at the engine debounce during a busy run) plus IPC transfer and re-renders of a pane the user cannot see, for as long as the maximize persists - the FINDING-013 class resurfacing on the one hiding surface the spec amendment did not enumerate. The trigger x state matrix, TEST-446/447 and e2e TEST-466 cover only the two named hosts; no vector exercises maximize. Spec-origin: the implementation matches the amended letter (both named hosts are covered), so no contract flag.
fix: Thread the third surface into the same boundary: PaneTile passes hidden={wsInactive || (the workspace has a maximized pane that is not this pane)} - it already knows isMax; add the maximized-sibling half - and amend REQ-010 to enumerate maximize-over as a hidden host with matrix vectors (own-root notification while maximized-over fetches zero and marks stale; un-maximize fetches exactly once iff stale). The FINDING-013 proposed CONV (key on EVERY keep-mounted-but-hidden host) already covers this class - doc-sync should promote it naming maximize explicitly.
location: src/renderer/index.css:278-287 (.ws-max sibling hiding) + src/renderer/components/PaneTile.tsx:48,155 vs 02-spec.md REQ-010 (displayed model)
resolution: Mini-loopback (spec iteration 3): REQ-010 amended to the THREE-host displayed model (maximized-over via .ws-max added); PaneTile derives self-excluded maximizedOver from s.maximized[wsId] and threads hidden={wsInactive || maximizedOver}; TEST-468 structurally pins both. All gates re-derived green.

claim: The FINDING-020 fix is fragile at both ends of the remount boundary. (a) Code, dev-only: main.tsx wraps App in StrictMode, whose development double effect invocation defeats the merged effect - setup #1 consumes the wasHidden evidence by flipping entry.hidden to false, so setup #2 sees a non-hidden, non-inFlight entry and fetches: a NON-stale restore fetches once in development where the amended matrix pins ZERO (stale restore and fresh mount stay correct via the inFlight guard). Production StrictMode does not double-invoke, so no shipped-count violation - but the effect is contractually expected to be idempotent under React re-invocation, and a future keep-mounted remount (Activity/offscreen) would manifest it for real. (b) Tests: the amended REQ-010 acceptance demands restore vectors driven through the REAL component lifecycle with a registryDetail call-count spy, but no such vector exists anywhere - TEST-450 is a substring grep (any getState( reference in the file satisfies it), TEST-447/423 pin the slice seam beneath the component, and the e2e additions (TEST-464/465/466) cover workspace ACTIVATION (a no-remount prop flip), never the minimize-restore REMOUNT - so a regression of the exact FINDING-020 defect (mount effect fetching unconditionally) would pass the entire frozen suite. The two-way structural pinning was a sanctioned harness compromise per the loopback direction; recorded so the residual stays a decision, not an accident.
fix: Make the effect double-invocation-safe: capture the hidden-to-displayed classification once per mount in a ref (initialize wasHidden from a useRef before the first setOrkyPaneHidden call) so a StrictMode re-invocation cannot re-classify the mount as a fresh T1 bind; and add the missing real-lifecycle vector - an e2e (or jsdom component) minimize-then-restore test with a main-side registryDetail call counter pinning 0 (non-stale) / 1 (stale).

claim: Two theme/status CSS variables consumed pre-F9 are referenced but never DECLARED anywhere in the theme system: --status-failure (index.css:59,65) and --status-needs-input (DecisionQueuePanel.tsx:158, StatusBar.tsx:110, index.css:311-312) always render their hard-coded var() fallback and no theme override can ever reach them. Discovered as a byproduct of F9's TEST-462 repo-wide theme-var-validity scan (04-tests.md Scope note 1), which had to special-case both as 'established' (referenced-anywhere, not declared-anywhere) so the scan didn't false-positive on pre-existing, out-of-F9-scope code. Not F9's to fix — filed as a backlog item for whichever feature next touches theme.ts's var map or these two consumers.
fix: When theme.ts's declared var map (or a future theming feature) is next touched, either declare --status-failure/--status-needs-input in the map (giving them real per-theme override support like --status-needs already has) or fold their consumers onto the already-declared --status-failure/--status-needs equivalents if that was always the intent; re-run TEST-462 with the established-name rule tightened back to declared-only once resolved.
